name: app.R
content:
# Stat 542 Third Place Project Shiny App

# Load packages
library(tidyverse)
library(shiny)
library(readr)

# Data
spaces_df <- read_csv("https://raw.githubusercontent.com/iofeii/class_data/refs/heads/main/third_place_index_1.0.csv")

spaces_df <- spaces_df %>%
  mutate(
    geoid = str_pad(as.character(geoid), 11, pad = "0"),
    geoid.stco = substr(geoid, 1, 5)
  )

app_data <- spaces_df %>%
  group_by(geoid.stco) %>%
  summarize(
    enviro_class = names(sort(table(urban_rural_class), decreasing = TRUE))[1],  # since the og data is at tract level, there may be multiple classes per county, so pick the most frequent
    avg.percent = mean(third_place_percentile, na.rm = TRUE),
    pop = sum(total_population, na.rm = TRUE),
    income = mean(median_income, na.rm = TRUE),
    under_18 = sum(pop_under_18, na.rm = TRUE) / sum(total_population, na.rm = TRUE) * 100,
    num_places = sum(total_places, na.rm = TRUE),
    num_free_places =
      sum(place_of_worship_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(library_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(social_facility_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(community_centre_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(park_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(garden_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(square_count, na.rm = TRUE) +
      sum(village_green_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    pct_free_places = num_free_places / num_places
  )

vars <- c(
  "Avg Percent" = "avg.percent",
  "Population" = "pop",
  "Median Income" = "income",
  "Percent Under 18" = "under_18",
  "Number of Places" = "num_places",
  "Number of Free Places" = "num_free_places",
  "Percent Free Places" = "pct_free_places"
)

# ================
# UI
# ================

ui <- fluidPage(
  titlePanel("Third Place Data Scatterplot"),
  
  sidebarLayout(
    sidebarPanel(
      selectInput('xcol', 'X Variable', vars),
      selectInput('ycol', 'Y Variable', vars),
      
      # Checkbox for user to select which enviro (categorical variable)
      checkboxGroupInput(
        'enviro',
        'Select Environment:',
        choices = unique(app_data$enviro_class),
        selected = unique(app_data$enviro_class)
      )
    ),
    
    mainPanel(
      plotOutput("plot")
    )
  )
)

# ================
# Server
# ================

server <- function(input, output) {
  
  output$plot <- renderPlot({
    
    # Filter the dataset based on user-selected enviro
    ds <- app_data %>%
      filter(enviro_class %in% input$enviro)
    
    # Create scatterplot using selected variables
    ggplot(ds, aes(x = .data[[input$xcol]], y = .data[[input$ycol]], color = enviro_class)) +
      geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
      labs(
        # Title that updates dynamically
        title = paste(input$ycol, "vs", input$xcol),
        color = "Environment"
      )
  })
}

# ================
# Server
# ================
shinyApp(ui, server)
